fields.description:
## Why
The declarative descriptors + the generic chunker/router are the engine of the gate; production prompts live
in the workflow-engine prompt library (not the experiment JSON).

## What
- criteria_v8 descriptors -> production prompt library (resolve_prompt / prompts-as-files).
- Generic orchestrator: applies_at level/type routing; facet chunker (base_chunk x size_factor); exec-tier
  dispatch (single-turn / 2-step / agent); overlay routing (deterministic + LLM-router).

## Scope
- NO TRUNCATION BY DESIGN, and NEVER CHUNK TICKET CONTENT. The reviewed plan/ticket is ALWAYS whole — a cap
  or a content-split reviews a plan that is not the one in rebar. The lever to fit context is the RUBRIC side,
  the MODEL, and (for container criteria) the per-child pairing — never the content.
- SIZE-HANDLING ORDER (content always whole): (1) run criteria in BATCHES per config; (2) if that hits — or is
  calculated to exceed — the context limit, fall back to ONE CRITERION PER CALL (full content, minimal rubric);
  (3) if still over, ESCALATE to a higher-context model; (4) if the ticket is STILL too big for the largest
  window, that is itself a FAILURE FINDING — the ticket is too large to review in full and the agent must
  reduce/decompose it (the extreme of P4 oversize / G5 decomposition). No content chunking; no silent
  INDETERMINATE-for-size.
- CONTAINER COVERAGE (G3 child-coverage, G4 child-consistency) — parent + children, ONE CHILD AT A TIME:
  evaluate (parent + a single child, both WHOLE) per call and accumulate deterministically (G3: union of the
  parent ACs each child covers → the uncovered set; G4: the interaction modes per pair/cluster). If any
  (parent + child) pairing is too big even for the escalated window, that is a FAILURE FINDING — the
  ticket(s) are too large, reduce them. Never truncate a child.
- MISSING-INFO / ABSENCE false-positives: because content is NEVER chunked, a finder applying batched (or
  single) criteria always sees the WHOLE plan — so no partial-view FP from rubric-batching. The only sliced
  view is G3/G4 one-child-at-a-time; there, an absence / 'missing X' finding is a CANDIDATE — Pass-2 confirms
  X is absent from the COMPLETE set (parent + ALL children, via retrieval) before it stands (absence findings:
  higher bar; the verifier must have whole-artifact access).
- ISF (intent-source fidelity) SUPPORTING context: the primary plan is never reduced, but the linked SESSION
  LOG is supporting context — if it exceeds the escalated window, SUMMARIZE it and run ISF against the summary,
  RECORDING that the check used a summary (not the full log); the finding then carries REDUCED CONFIDENCE. A
  reduced-confidence intent check beats none. (This summarization is for the supporting log ONLY, never the plan.)
- Coverage (model/window used, batch→single fallback, summary-based ISF, any too-big failure finding) is
  RECORDED on the attestation so the review's completeness is visible.
- Built ON the workflow engine; the exact SHAPE (gate-as-workflow vs bespoke; dynamic fan-out handling)
  depends on the `6f2d` (soupy-spool-revel) brainstorm this epic depends_on. Reuse rebar.llm extension points.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Registry loads from the prompt library; the coverage guard passes.
- [ ] Orchestrator filters via applies_at, packs by facet, dispatches by exec tier.
- [ ] No criterion greps the codebase except the code-grounding set (E4/G1G2/A1/G6).
- [ ] The orchestrator reviews the FULL plan + children — NO TRUNCATION and NO CONTENT-CHUNKING by design. Size order: batch criteria → ONE criterion/call → escalate to a higher-context model → (still too big) a FAILURE FINDING that the ticket must be reduced. Coverage recorded.
- [ ] Container criteria (G3/G4) evaluate parent + ONE child at a time (both whole) and aggregate; a too-big (parent+child) pairing is a failure finding (reduce the ticket(s)); absence findings from the per-child view are verified against the COMPLETE set before they stand.
- [ ] Context-window escalation: estimate tokens / catch context-limit errors → escalate up a configured model-by-window ladder (after dropping to one criterion per call, before declaring the ticket too big). ISF on a too-large session log falls back to a documented SUMMARY with reduced confidence.

## Acceptance Criteria (added — T8 DET execution path)
- [ ] The exec-dispatch covers the DETERMINISTIC tier: `exec=DET` criteria (the P1–P8 floor) run via a CODE executor alongside the 1-TURN / 2-STEP / AGENT (LLM) tiers — the DET tier has an explicit execution path (invoking the DET floor 012e), not just the LLM tiers.
